# Data Model — Supabase Postgres Schema

This is an important finding. The brief locks "Algeria's 58 wilayas," but as of November 2025 Algeria officially has 69 wilayas. I'll design the schema so the wilaya table is data-driven (not a hardcoded constant) and flag this discrepancy. I have enough verified detail to write the section now.

## Data Model — Supabase Postgres Schema

> **Verification note (read first):** The brief locks "58 wilayas," but Algeria officially expanded to **69 wilayas** on 16 Nov 2025 (11 new provinces in the Hauts Plateaux/south) — confirmed by [Provinces of Algeria, Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Provinces_of_Algeria) and [Morocco World News](https://www.moroccoworldnews.com/2025/11/268183/). I therefore model `wilaya` as a **seeded data table, not a hardcoded `CHECK (1..58)`**, so a migration can add the 11 new codes without a schema change. Use the maintained dataset [S450R1/algeria-cities-2025](https://github.com/S450R1/algeria-cities-2025) (69 wilayas + communes, SQL/JSON) for seeding. Chargily webhook fields/signature verified against [dev.chargily.com/pay-v2/webhooks](https://dev.chargily.com/pay-v2/webhooks). Overlap-prevention approach verified against [PostgreSQL exclusion constraints / btree_gist](https://www.cybertec-postgresql.com/en/postgresql-exclusion-constraints-beyond-unique/).

All tables live in `public`. PKs are `uuid default gen_random_uuid()` except seed/master tables (smallint/text natural keys). `id`/`created_at timestamptz default now()`/`updated_at timestamptz default now()` are implied on every transactional table (`updated_at` maintained by a shared `set_updated_at()` trigger). Soft-delete via `deleted_at timestamptz` where noted. `app_user.id` mirrors `auth.users.id` (Supabase Auth).

### Enum types

```sql
create type user_role         as enum ('guest','host_individual','host_hotel','hotel_staff','admin','super_admin');
create type host_type          as enum ('individual','hotel');
create type staff_role         as enum ('reception','manager');         -- within a hotel
create type property_status    as enum ('draft','pending','approved','rejected','suspended');
create type listing_kind       as enum ('single_unit','multi_room');     -- home vs hotel; drives RoomType logic
create type booking_status     as enum ('pending','confirmed','cancelled','completed');
create type payment_status     as enum ('pending','processing','paid','failed','refunded','partially_refunded','expired');
create type payment_method     as enum ('chargily_edahabia','chargily_cib','baridi_pay');
create type payout_status      as enum ('scheduled','requested','processing','paid','failed','on_hold');
create type review_status      as enum ('pending','published','hidden','removed');
create type dispute_status     as enum ('open','under_review','resolved','rejected','cancelled');
create type cancellation_policy as enum ('flexible','moderate','strict');
create type notification_type  as enum (
  'booking_requested','booking_confirmed','booking_cancelled','booking_reminder',
  'payment_succeeded','payment_failed','payout_paid',
  'review_received','message_received','listing_approved','listing_rejected',
  'dispute_opened','dispute_resolved');
create type conversation_kind  as enum ('booking','inquiry','support');
type_note: 'pricing_rule_kind' as enum ('weekend','seasonal','long_stay','manual');
```

I/O note for the API layer: enums serialize as text to PostgREST. For columns that may churn (`notification_type`, `amenity` categories) a check-constrained `text` is also acceptable; the booking/payment/status enums above are **stable contracts** and stay as real enums.

### Master / seed tables

| table | columns | notes |
|---|---|---|
| `wilaya` | `code smallint pk` (1–58 today, 1–69 after migration), `name_ar text not null`, `name_fr text not null`, `name_en text`, `ar_slug text`, `lat numeric(9,6)`, `lng numeric(9,6)`, `is_active boolean default true` | Seeded, **not** range-checked. `is_active` lets you ship 58 now and flip on the 11 new ones later. |
| `commune` | `id integer pk`, `wilaya_code smallint not null references wilaya(code)`, `name_ar`, `name_fr`, `name_en`, `post_code text` | ~1,541 rows. Index on `wilaya_code`. |
| `property_type` | `id smallint pk`, `slug text unique not null` (`apartment`,`villa`,`riad`,`studio`,`hotel`,`guesthouse`,`chalet`,`bungalow`), `name_ar/fr/en`, `kind listing_kind not null`, `icon text`, `sort_order smallint` | `kind` defaults a property to single_unit vs multi_room at creation. |
| `amenity` | `id smallint pk`, `slug text unique`, `category text` (`general`,`kitchen`,`bathroom`,`safety`,`accessibility`,`outdoor`), `name_ar/fr/en`, `icon text` | e.g. `wifi`,`ac`,`heating`,`parking`,`pool`,`elevator`,`hammam`,`prayer_room`,`generator`. |
| `cancellation_policy_def` | `tier cancellation_policy pk`, `refund_full_until_hours int`, `refund_partial_pct int`, `name_ar/fr/en`, `description_ar/fr/en` | Drives refund math in code; keeps copy localized + editable. |

### Identity, host, staff

**`app_user`** — `id uuid pk references auth.users(id) on delete cascade`, `full_name text`, `display_name text`, `phone text` (E.164, Algeria `+213`), `phone_verified boolean default false`, `avatar_url text`, `preferred_locale text default 'ar' check (preferred_locale in ('ar','fr','en'))`, `default_wilaya_code smallint references wilaya(code)`, `is_host boolean default false`, `is_active boolean default true`, `created_at`, `updated_at`. Profile mirror of Auth; one account = guest + host.

**`user_role_grant`** — `id uuid pk`, `user_id uuid not null references app_user(id) on delete cascade`, `role user_role not null`, `granted_by uuid references app_user(id)`, `granted_at timestamptz default now()`, `unique(user_id, role)`. Roles are additive grants (an account can hold `guest`+`host_individual`); RLS reads from here, not a single column.

**`host_profile`** — `id uuid pk`, `user_id uuid not null unique references app_user(id) on delete cascade`, `host_type host_type not null`, `legal_name text`, `display_name text not null`, `bio_ar/fr/en text`, `id_doc_type text` (`cni`,`passport`,`permis`), `id_doc_url text` (Storage path), `rc_number text` (Registre de Commerce — hotels), `nif text` (tax id), `verified_status text default 'unverified' check (verified_status in ('unverified','pending','verified','rejected'))`, `payout_method payment_method`, `ccp_rip text` (Algérie Poste CCP/RIP for payouts), `bank_rib text`, `commission_rate_bps int default 1000 check (commission_rate_bps between 0 and 5000)` (per-host override of platform default; bps = basis points), `created_at`, `updated_at`. `host_type` distinguishes individual vs hotel for verification + listing flow.

**`hotel_staff`** — `id uuid pk`, `host_profile_id uuid not null references host_profile(id) on delete cascade`, `user_id uuid not null references app_user(id) on delete cascade`, `staff_role staff_role not null`, `is_active boolean default true`, `unique(host_profile_id, user_id)`. Lets reception/manager accounts act on a hotel's properties without owning the host_profile.

### Property & rooms

**`property`** — `id uuid pk`, `host_profile_id uuid not null references host_profile(id) on delete restrict`, `property_type_id smallint not null references property_type(id)`, `listing_kind listing_kind not null` (denormalized from property_type at insert, but overridable), `title_ar/fr/en text`, `description_ar/fr/en text`, `status property_status not null default 'draft'`, `wilaya_code smallint not null references wilaya(code)`, `commune_id integer references commune(id)`, `address_line text`, `lat numeric(9,6)`, `lng numeric(9,6)`, `geo geography(Point,4326)` (generated/maintained from lat/lng for radius search via PostGIS), `cancellation_tier cancellation_policy not null default 'moderate'`, `checkin_time time default '14:00'`, `checkout_time time default '12:00'`, `house_rules_ar/fr/en text`, `instant_book boolean default false`, `currency char(3) default 'DZD' check (currency = 'DZD')` (v1 lock), `min_nights int default 1 check (min_nights >= 1)`, `max_nights int`, `cover_image_url text`, `rating_avg numeric(3,2) default 0 check (rating_avg between 0 and 5)`, `review_count int default 0`, `published_at timestamptz`, `deleted_at timestamptz`, `created_at`, `updated_at`. Status lifecycle `draft→pending→approved/rejected/suspended` enforced in code + an `is_publicly_visible` derived predicate (`status='approved' and deleted_at is null`) used by RLS read policies.

**`property_review_log`** (admin moderation trail) — `id uuid pk`, `property_id uuid not null references property(id) on delete cascade`, `actor_id uuid references app_user(id)`, `from_status property_status`, `to_status property_status not null`, `reason text`, `created_at`. Append-only audit of every status transition.

**`room_type`** — `id uuid pk`, `property_id uuid not null references property(id) on delete cascade`, `name_ar/fr/en text`, `is_default boolean default false`, `max_occupancy smallint not null check (max_occupancy > 0)`, `max_adults smallint`, `max_children smallint`, `beds jsonb default '[]'` (`[{type:'double',count:1}]`), `size_sqm smallint`, `base_price_minor bigint not null check (base_price_minor >= 0)` (DZD in **centimes**, integer minor units — see Money), `weekend_price_minor bigint`, `cleaning_fee_minor bigint default 0`, `extra_guest_fee_minor bigint default 0`, `inventory_count smallint not null default 1 check (inventory_count >= 1)`, `is_active boolean default true`, `sort_order smallint`, `created_at`, `updated_at`. **Partial unique index** `unique (property_id) where is_default` guarantees exactly one default unit.

**`room_amenity`** (room-level extras, optional) and **`property_amenity`** join — `property_amenity(property_id uuid references property(id) on delete cascade, amenity_id smallint references amenity(id), primary key(property_id, amenity_id))`. Same shape for `room_amenity(room_type_id, amenity_id)`.

**`property_image`** — `id uuid pk`, `property_id uuid not null references property(id) on delete cascade`, `room_type_id uuid references room_type(id) on delete set null`, `storage_path text not null`, `alt_ar/fr/en text`, `sort_order smallint default 0`, `is_cover boolean default false`, `created_at`.

#### Single-home vs hotel modeling

- **Single home (`listing_kind='single_unit'`):** exactly **one** `room_type` row, created automatically when the property is created, `is_default=true`, `inventory_count=1`, name auto-filled ("Entire place"/«Le logement entier»/«السكن كامل»). The guest never picks a room; the UI hides room selection. Pricing/occupancy/availability all hang off that one row — uniform with hotels, so booking/availability code has **one code path**.
- **Hotel (`listing_kind='multi_room'`):** many `room_type` rows, each with `inventory_count > 1` (e.g. "Standard Double" ×12). A booking targets a `room_type` and consumes one of its `inventory_count` units per night.

### Availability & inventory

I use **per-date rows** (one row per room_type per calendar day), not date ranges. Rationale: hotels need per-night `units_open`, `price_override`, and close-outs; per-date rows make availability lookups, price overrides, and remaining-inventory math trivial and index-friendly, and avoid range-splitting on partial bookings. Range storage is reserved for the **booking** record itself (where exclusion constraints apply).

**`availability`** — `id uuid pk`, `room_type_id uuid not null references room_type(id) on delete cascade`, `date date not null`, `units_open smallint not null check (units_open >= 0)` (defaults to room_type.inventory_count via app/seed), `units_booked smallint not null default 0 check (units_booked >= 0)`, `price_override_minor bigint check (price_override_minor >= 0)` (null ⇒ fall back to room_type base/weekend price + pricing_rule), `min_stay smallint check (min_stay >= 1)`, `max_stay smallint`, `is_closed boolean not null default false` (close-out: no arrivals/stays this date), `closed_to_arrival boolean default false` (CTA), `closed_to_departure boolean default false` (CTD), `updated_at`, **`unique(room_type_id, date)`**, `check (units_booked <= units_open)`. Hot lookups hit `(room_type_id, date)`.

**`pricing_rule`** (optional layered pricing) — `id uuid pk`, `room_type_id uuid not null references room_type(id) on delete cascade`, `kind pricing_rule_kind not null`, `date_range daterange`, `dow_mask smallint` (bitmask Mon–Sun for weekend rules), `min_nights_threshold smallint` (long-stay), `adjust_type text check (adjust_type in ('percent','absolute'))`, `adjust_value_minor bigint`, `priority smallint default 0`, `is_active boolean default true`. `availability.price_override_minor` (manual) wins over rules; rules win over `room_type` base — resolved at quote time.

#### Preventing double-booking & overlap

Two complementary layers (per verified sources):

1. **Inventory counter (handles multi-unit room types).** Booking creation runs in a transaction that takes a **transaction-level advisory lock** keyed on the room_type (`pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtextextended(room_type_id::text, 0))`), re-checks every night `units_booked < units_open AND NOT is_closed` across the requested span, then increments `units_booked` per date. The lock serializes concurrent attempts on the same room type so two requests can't both grab the last unit. A `CHECK (units_booked <= units_open)` is the backstop.

2. **Exclusion constraint (single-unit guarantee + integrity).** For `inventory_count = 1` room types, an overlap is a hard error at the DB layer. I store the booking span as a range and add, via `btree_gist`:

```sql
create extension if not exists btree_gist;
alter table booking add column stay_range daterange
  generated always as (daterange(check_in, check_out, '[)')) stored;
-- one unit can't be double-booked for overlapping nights:
alter table booking add constraint booking_no_overlap
  exclude using gist (room_type_id with =, stay_range with &&)
  where (status in ('pending','confirmed') and unit_index = 0);
```

For multi-unit hotels, overlap alone isn't sufficient (12 units can overlap legitimately), so the **advisory-lock + counter** path is authoritative there; `unit_index` (0..inventory_count-1) lets us still apply the exclusion constraint per assigned unit if we later pin guests to specific physical rooms (v2). `daterange '[)'` = inclusive check-in, exclusive check-out, so back-to-back stays (checkout day = next checkin day) do **not** collide.

### Booking, payment, payout

**`booking`** — `id uuid pk`, `code text unique not null` (human ref, e.g. `BK-2026-7F3A2`), `property_id uuid not null references property(id) on delete restrict`, `room_type_id uuid not null references room_type(id) on delete restrict`, `guest_id uuid not null references app_user(id) on delete restrict`, `host_profile_id uuid not null references host_profile(id)`, `check_in date not null`, `check_out date not null`, `stay_range daterange generated`, `nights int generated always as (check_out - check_in) stored check (nights > 0)`, `adults smallint not null default 1 check (adults > 0)`, `children smallint not null default 0`, `units smallint not null default 1 check (units > 0)`, `unit_index smallint default 0`, `status booking_status not null default 'pending'`, `currency char(3) default 'DZD'`, `nightly_subtotal_minor bigint not null`, `cleaning_fee_minor bigint default 0`, `extra_guest_fee_minor bigint default 0`, `discount_minor bigint default 0`, `taxes_minor bigint default 0`, `service_fee_minor bigint default 0` (guest-side platform fee), `total_minor bigint not null check (total_minor >= 0)`, `commission_minor bigint not null default 0` (platform cut from host), `host_payout_minor bigint not null default 0` (= total − commission − guest-side fees as applicable), `cancellation_tier cancellation_policy not null`, `cancelled_by uuid references app_user(id)`, `cancellation_reason text`, `cancelled_at timestamptz`, `refund_minor bigint default 0`, `confirmed_at timestamptz`, `completed_at timestamptz`, `special_requests text`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `check (check_out > check_in)`. Status `pending→confirmed` (after payment or instant_book) `→completed` (after checkout) / `→cancelled`.

Money breakdown is **stored on the booking** (snapshot at purchase) so later price/commission changes don't rewrite history.

**`transaction`** — `id uuid pk`, `booking_id uuid references booking(id) on delete set null`, `kind text not null check (kind in ('payment','refund','payout','chargeback'))`, `method payment_method not null`, `status payment_status not null default 'pending'`, `amount_minor bigint not null check (amount_minor >= 0)`, `currency char(3) default 'DZD'`, `provider text check (provider in ('chargily','baridi_pay'))`, `provider_checkout_id text` (Chargily `id`), `provider_invoice_id text` (Chargily `invoice_id`), `provider_payment_method text` (Chargily `payment_method`: `edahabia`/`cib`), `provider_fees_minor bigint default 0` (Chargily `fees`), `checkout_url text` (Chargily `url`), `success_url text`, `failure_url text`, `idempotency_key text unique`, `raw_payload jsonb` (last webhook body), `paid_at timestamptz`, `created_at`, `updated_at`. One booking can have several rows (initial payment, later refund). `provider_checkout_id` indexed unique-where-not-null for webhook reconciliation.

**`payment_webhook_event`** (idempotent ingestion) — `id uuid pk`, `provider text not null`, `event_type text not null` (Chargily: `checkout.paid`/`checkout.failed`/`checkout.canceled`), `provider_event_id text`, `signature text`, `signature_valid boolean`, `payload jsonb not null`, `transaction_id uuid references transaction(id)`, `processed boolean default false`, `received_at timestamptz default now()`, `unique(provider, provider_event_id)`. Edge Function verifies the `signature` header (HMAC-SHA256 over raw body with the API secret — per Chargily docs) before mutating any `transaction`/`booking`; the unique constraint dedupes retries.

**`payout`** — `id uuid pk`, `host_profile_id uuid not null references host_profile(id)`, `status payout_status not null default 'scheduled'`, `amount_minor bigint not null check (amount_minor >= 0)`, `currency char(3) default 'DZD'`, `method payment_method`, `destination_rib text`, `destination_ccp text`, `period_start date`, `period_end date`, `reference text`, `paid_at timestamptz`, `failure_reason text`, `created_at`, `updated_at`. **`payout_item`** join — `payout_id uuid references payout(id) on delete cascade`, `booking_id uuid references booking(id)`, `amount_minor bigint`, `primary key(payout_id, booking_id)` — links each payout to the bookings it settles (sum of `host_payout_minor`). Algerian payouts are typically off-platform bank/CCP transfers, so payout status is operator-driven; the model records the ledger, not an automated rail (v1).

### Reviews, messaging, social, ops

**`review`** — `id uuid pk`, `booking_id uuid not null unique references booking(id) on delete cascade` (one review per stay), `property_id uuid not null references property(id) on delete cascade`, `author_id uuid not null references app_user(id)`, `target text not null check (target in ('property','guest'))` (two-way: guest→property, host→guest), `status review_status not null default 'pending'`, `overall smallint not null check (overall between 1 and 5)`, `cleanliness smallint check (cleanliness between 1 and 5)`, `accuracy smallint`, `communication smallint`, `location smallint`, `value smallint`, `checkin smallint`, `comment_text text`, `host_reply text`, `host_reply_at timestamptz`, `published_at timestamptz`, `created_at`, `updated_at`. Category columns mirror Airbnb's six axes. `overall` is the guest-entered headline score; category averages are aggregated separately (below).

**`conversation`** — `id uuid pk`, `kind conversation_kind not null`, `property_id uuid references property(id) on delete set null`, `booking_id uuid references booking(id) on delete set null`, `guest_id uuid not null references app_user(id)`, `host_profile_id uuid not null references host_profile(id)`, `last_message_at timestamptz`, `created_at`, `unique(booking_id) where booking_id is not null`. **`message`** — `id uuid pk`, `conversation_id uuid not null references conversation(id) on delete cascade`, `sender_id uuid not null references app_user(id)`, `body text`, `attachment_path text`, `read_at timestamptz`, `created_at`. Realtime subscribes to `message` filtered by `conversation_id`.

**`favorite`** — `user_id uuid references app_user(id) on delete cascade`, `property_id uuid references property(id) on delete cascade`, `created_at`, `primary key(user_id, property_id)`.

**`dispute`** — `id uuid pk`, `booking_id uuid not null references booking(id) on delete cascade`, `opened_by uuid not null references app_user(id)`, `against uuid references app_user(id)`, `category text check (category in ('refund','no_show','property_mismatch','damage','payment','other'))`, `status dispute_status not null default 'open'`, `description text`, `resolution_note text`, `resolved_by uuid references app_user(id)`, `resolved_at timestamptz`, `refund_minor bigint default 0`, `created_at`, `updated_at`. **`dispute_message`**/`dispute_evidence` (storage paths) hang off it.

**`notification`** — `id uuid pk`, `user_id uuid not null references app_user(id) on delete cascade`, `type notification_type not null`, `title_ar/fr/en text`, `body_ar/fr/en text`, `data jsonb` (deep-link payload, e.g. `{booking_id}`), `read_at timestamptz`, `sent_push boolean default false`, `created_at`. Localized columns so the mobile app renders in the user's `preferred_locale` without a translation round-trip.

### Derived / aggregate fields (rating freshness)

- **Per-property `rating_avg` + `review_count`** are **denormalized columns on `property`**, maintained by an `AFTER INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE` trigger `trg_review_rollup` on `review` that recomputes `avg(overall)`/`count(*)` over `status='published', target='property'` for that property. Denormalized (not a view) because search results sort/filter by rating and must be index-backed — a view would re-aggregate on every search.
- **Category averages + overall review score** live in a **materialized view** `property_review_stats` (`property_id, avg_cleanliness, avg_accuracy, avg_communication, avg_location, avg_value, avg_checkin, computed_overall, review_count`). `computed_overall` = mean of the six category averages. Refreshed `REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY` on a schedule (pg_cron / Supabase scheduled Edge Function, e.g. every 15 min) — these feed the property detail page, not search ranking, so slight staleness is fine and avoids per-write recompute cost. (Use the lightweight trigger for the two hot fields; the MV for the six rarely-read category breakdowns.)
- **Host aggregate** (`response_rate`, `acceptance_rate`) → same MV pattern, refreshed nightly.

### Indexing plan (hot queries)

```sql
-- Search: by wilaya + dates + guests + type, visible only
create index ix_property_search on property (wilaya_code, property_type_id, status)
  where status = 'approved' and deleted_at is null;
create index ix_property_rating on property (wilaya_code, rating_avg desc)
  where status = 'approved';
create index ix_property_geo on property using gist (geo);          -- radius / map search
-- Availability lookup (does this room have N open nights in range?)
create unique index ux_avail_room_date on availability (room_type_id, date);
create index ix_avail_open on availability (room_type_id, date)
  where is_closed = false;
-- Booking overlap / host reservations
create index ix_booking_overlap on booking using gist (room_type_id, stay_range);
create index ix_booking_host on booking (host_profile_id, status, check_in desc);
create index ix_booking_guest on booking (guest_id, status, created_at desc);
create index ix_booking_property_dates on booking (property_id, check_in, check_out);
-- Payments / webhooks reconciliation
create unique index ux_txn_checkout on transaction (provider_checkout_id)
  where provider_checkout_id is not null;
-- Messaging realtime + inbox
create index ix_msg_conv on message (conversation_id, created_at desc);
create index ix_conv_host on conversation (host_profile_id, last_message_at desc);
-- Reviews rollup source
create index ix_review_property on review (property_id) where status = 'published' and target = 'property';
-- Notifications unread badge
create index ix_notif_unread on notification (user_id, created_at desc) where read_at is null;
```

The search flow: filter `property` by `(wilaya_code, type, guests≤max_occupancy)` → for the date span, exclude properties whose every room_type lacks `units_open - units_booked >= requested` on any night (anti-join against `availability` / `booking`). Geo radius search uses the GiST `geo` index when the user searches "near me."

### Money handling

- **Integer minor units (`bigint`), DZD centimes** — every monetary column is `*_minor bigint`. DZD has a (nominal) 2-decimal subunit; storing centimes avoids float drift and keeps arithmetic exact. `bigint` headroom covers large hotel/long-stay totals. Display layer divides by 100 and formats with Western Arabic numerals (`1234`) + `دج`/`DZD`. A `CHECK (currency = 'DZD')` enforces the v1 single-currency lock everywhere.
- **Commission, fees, taxes placement:** computed and **snapshotted on `booking`** at purchase — `commission_minor` (host-side platform cut, derived from `host_profile.commission_rate_bps`), `service_fee_minor` (optional guest-side fee), `cleaning_fee_minor`, `extra_guest_fee_minor`, `taxes_minor` (placeholder for any future TVA/tourist tax — kept as an explicit column so a tax rule can populate it without schema change), `host_payout_minor` (what the host is owed). The `transaction` table records the actual money moved (incl. Chargily `provider_fees_minor`); `payout`/`payout_item` settle `host_payout_minor` to hosts. Keeping the breakdown on the booking = immutable financial record independent of later rate edits.

### v2 deferrals (modeled-for, not built)

- **Multi-room single booking** (one reservation spanning several room_types/units) — current `booking` is one room_type per row; v2 adds a `booking_group` parent + line items. `unit_index` already reserves the per-physical-room dimension.
- **iCal / channel-manager sync** (Booking.com/Airbnb import-export) — reserve `availability.source text` + an `ical_feed` table; not in v1.
- **Loyalty / wallet / promo codes** — `discount_minor` column exists, but `promo_code`/`wallet_ledger` tables are deferred.
- **Multi-currency** — `currency` columns + `CHECK='DZD'` make relaxing to multi-currency a constraint drop + FX table later.
- **The 11 new wilayas** — seed 58 active now; `wilaya.is_active` + data-driven design lets ops enable codes 59–69 with an INSERT, no migration.
